title: Modern Nightstands
summary: This modern nightstand has clean architectural lines with a linear look.
description: This modern nightstand has clean architectural lines with a linear look. It has a single drawer and features inset panels on the top, sides, and drawer front. These plans will build two complete nightstands.

materials.name: 12" drawer slides (purchase ones that require 0.5” clearance on each sides)

steps.title: NOTE:
steps.body: Repeat each of these steps to build two nightstands. Cut list provides measurements for two nightstands.

steps.title: BUILD SIDE FRAMES
steps.body: Drill pocket holes on each end of the HORIZONTAL SIDE SPANS using the 1-1/2” jig setting. Attach HORIZONTAL SIDE SPANS to legs at the locations shown on the diagram with wood glue and 2-1/2” pocket-hole screws. The pocket holes for the top piece should face downward as indicated on the diagram.

steps.title: ATTACH SIDE INSERTS
steps.body: Drill pocket holes around the sides of the LOWER SIDE pieces as shown on the diagram using the 3/4" jig setting. Attach the LOWER SIDE pieces flush to the inside of the side frames using 1-1/4” pocket hole screws.

steps.title: ATTACH HORIZONTAL SPANS
steps.body: Drill pocket holes on the HORIZONTAL FRONT/BACK SPANS using the 1-1/2” jig setting. Attach in the same location as the SIDE SPANS using wood glue and 2-1/2” pocket-hole screws. The pocket holes for the top piece should face downward as indicated on the diagram.

steps.title: ATTACH SHELF/TOPS
steps.body: Drill pocket holes on the underside of each SHELF/TOP piece, using the 3/4" jig setting, approximately 6” apart around the perimeter. Attach shelf flush with the surrounding spans using 1-1/4” pocket-hole screws. Attach the tops in the same fashion, however, recess the top by 1/2" all the way around.

steps.title: ATTACH BACK
steps.body: Attach the BACK to the nightstand using a brad nailer and 1-1/4” nails around the perimeter of the board.

steps.title: BUILD DRAWER BOXES
steps.body: Drill pocket holes on the DRAWER FRONT/BACKS as shown (note that the pocket holes are on the back side of the back board). Attach the DRAWER BOX SIDES to the DRAWER BOX FRONT/BACKS as shown in the diagram using wood glue and 1-1/4” pocket hole screws. Once the box frame is complete, attach the DRAWER BOTTOM using wood glue and 1-1/4” brad nails.

steps.title: INSTALL DRAWER SLIDES, BOX AND DRAWER FRONT
steps.body: Install the drawer slides so that they sit back 1.25” from the face edge of the nightstand. This will give a recessed inset panel look to the drawer. Drill two holes in the front of the drawer box about 6” from each side, centered top to bottom. You will use these holes to attach the drawer face from the inside of the box.

steps.title: INSTALL DRAWER FACE
steps.body: When installing a drawer face, it is a great idea to have some double-sided tape and playing cards on hand. You can space the drawer appropriately in the opening using the playing cards, and the double-sided tape will hold it in place until your ready to attach with screws from the back. When you have it exactly where you want it, use 1-1/4” pocket-hole screws to attach from the inside of the drawer into the drawer face.

steps.title: FINISHING
steps.body: Sand with 120 grit sandpaper, followed by 220 grit. Finish as desired with paint or stain.
